name: @finch.app/mcp-client
description: Connect Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers into Finch.

finch.name: MCP Client
finch.description: Connect to Model Context Protocol servers, expose their tools to the Finch, and provide an mcp.client capability for other extensions.
finch.systemPrompt: Use MCP with action=list/add/edit/remove to manage configured MCP services; never ask users to paste secrets in chat, because add/edit opens a secure form for secret values. To add an HTTP (url) MCP server just pass name+url — the form shows one API-token field and auto-creates a standard 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header; never pass, view, or hand-edit the token or any header, and only set authHeader for a non-standard header name (e.g. X-Api-Key). To use actual MCP server tools, call Finch ToolSearch with source:"mcp" first — mcp__<server>__<tool> names are never pre-loaded and will fail if called directly. ToolSearch connects matching servers, discovers their tools, and injects mcp__<server>__<tool> functions into the current run. Authorization can be granted per-tool (mcp__filesystem__read_file) or with a wildcard (mcp__filesystem__*).

promptGuides.title: Connect a filesystem MCP server
promptGuides.description: Ask Finch to help create a local servers.json config for filesystem access.
promptGuides.prompt: Help me configure MCP Client with a filesystem MCP server for my current project directory.
